entries.title: Newton's Second Law
entries.content: The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass. This is the most used law for solving force problems.
entries.example: A 10 kg object with 50 N net force accelerates at a = 50/10 = 5 m/s².

entries.title: Kinetic Energy
entries.content: Energy of motion. Depends on mass and the square of velocity — doubling speed quadruples kinetic energy.

entries.title: Ohm's Law
entries.content: Relates voltage, current, and resistance in an electrical circuit. Foundation for circuit analysis.
entries.example: 12 V battery, 4 Ω resistor → I = 12/4 = 3 A

entries.title: Wave Speed Equation
entries.content: Speed of a wave equals frequency times wavelength. Applies to sound, light, and water waves.

entries.title: Einstein Mass-Energy Equivalence
entries.content: Mass and energy are interchangeable. A tiny amount of mass converts to enormous energy — principle behind nuclear power.
